# DDx GraphQL Schema
# Single source of truth for the SvelteKit UI.
# Every type field is doc-commented. Relay cursor connections on all lists.

# ============================================
# Scalars
# ============================================

"""ISO-8601 datetime string"""
scalar DateTime

"""Arbitrary JSON blob (used for opaque map fields)"""
scalar JSON

# ============================================
# Interfaces
# ============================================

"""Node is the Relay global object identification interface"""
interface Node {
  """Globally unique identifier"""
  id: ID!
}

# ============================================
# Pagination (Relay Cursor Connections)
# ============================================

"""PageInfo carries cursor-based pagination state (Relay spec)"""
type PageInfo {
  """True when more edges exist after the last returned edge"""
  hasNextPage: Boolean!
  """True when more edges exist before the first returned edge"""
  hasPreviousPage: Boolean!
  """Cursor of the first edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  startCursor: String
  """Cursor of the last edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  endCursor: String
}

# ============================================
# Node & Project
# ============================================

"""NodeInfo represents the running ddx-server instance"""
type NodeInfo implements Node {
  """Unique node identifier (stable across restarts)"""
  id: ID!
  """Human-readable node name"""
  name: String!
  """Timestamp when the server process started"""
  startedAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the most recent heartbeat"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
}

"""Project represents a registered ddx project on this node"""
type Project implements Node {
  """Unique project identifier"""
  id: ID!
  """Short display name"""
  name: String!
  """Absolute filesystem path to the project root"""
  path: String!
  """Git remote URL, if configured"""
  gitRemote: String
  """When the project was first registered with the server"""
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the last successful probe"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
  """True when the project path is inaccessible"""
  unreachable: Boolean
  """Set when the project has been tombstoned (soft-deleted)"""
  tombstonedAt: DateTime
}

"""ProjectEdge is one edge in a ProjectConnection"""
type ProjectEdge {
  """The project at this position"""
  node: Project!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""ProjectConnection is a Relay cursor connection of projects"""
type ProjectConnection {
  """Ordered list of project edges"""
  edges: [ProjectEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of projects matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Beads
# ============================================

"""Bead is a portable work item with lifecycle metadata"""
type Bead implements Node {
  """Unique bead identifier (e.g. "ddx-a1b2c3d4")"""
  id: ID!
  """Short summary of the work"""
  title: String!
  """Lifecycle status: open, in-progress, closed, blocked"""
  status: String!
  """Numeric priority (lower is higher priority)"""
  priority: Int!
  """Issue classification (e.g. "task", "bug", "spike")"""
  issueType: String!
  """Agent or human currently responsible"""
  owner: String
  """When the bead was created"""
  createdAt: DateTime!
  """Who or what created the bead"""
  createdBy: String
  """Last modification timestamp"""
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  """Freeform labels for filtering and grouping"""
  labels: [String!]
  """Parent bead ID for hierarchy"""
  parent: String
  """Full description / body text"""
  description: String
  """Acceptance criteria text"""
  acceptance: String
  """Freeform notes appended during work"""
  notes: String
  """Dependency edges to other beads"""
  dependencies: [Dependency!]
}

"""Dependency is a directed edge between two beads"""
type Dependency {
  """The bead that has the dependency"""
  issueId: String!
  """The bead that must be completed first"""
  dependsOnId: String!
  """Dependency kind (e.g. "blocks", "relates-to")"""
  type: String!
  """When the dependency was created"""
  createdAt: String
  """Who created the dependency"""
  createdBy: String
  """Opaque metadata attached to the link"""
  metadata: String
}

"""BeadEdge is one edge in a BeadConnection"""
type BeadEdge {
  """The bead at this position"""
  node: Bead!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""BeadConnection is a Relay cursor connection of beads"""
type BeadConnection {
  """Ordered list of bead edges"""
  edges: [BeadEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of beads matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

"""BeadStatusCounts holds aggregate bead counts by status"""
type BeadStatusCounts {
  """Number of beads in open status"""
  open: Int!
  """Number of beads in closed status"""
  closed: Int!
  """Number of beads with unclosed dependencies"""
  blocked: Int!
  """Number of open beads whose dependencies are all closed"""
  ready: Int!
  """Total number of beads"""
  total: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Documents (library)
# ============================================

"""Document represents a docgraph document with dependency tracking"""
type Document implements Node {
  """Document identifier (usually the relative path)"""
  id: ID!
  """Filesystem path relative to the library root"""
  path: String!
  """Human-readable title extracted from frontmatter"""
  title: String!
  """IDs of documents this document depends on"""
  dependsOn: [String!]!
  """IDs of input artifacts consumed by this document"""
  inputs: [String!]!
  """IDs of documents that depend on this document"""
  dependents: [String!]!
  """Review state metadata (null if never reviewed)"""
  review: DocumentReview
  """True if parked (excluded from staleness checks)"""
  parkingLot: Boolean!
  """Prompt text embedded in the document frontmatter"""
  prompt: String
  """Execution definition embedded in the document (null if none)"""
  execDef: DocumentExecDef
}

"""DocumentReview holds review-state metadata for a document"""
type DocumentReview {
  """Hash of the document content at review time"""
  selfHash: String!
  """Map of dependency IDs to their hashes at review time"""
  deps: JSON!
  """ISO-8601 timestamp of the last review"""
  reviewedAt: String!
}

"""DocumentExecDef holds an execution definition embedded in a document"""
type DocumentExecDef {
  """Definition identifier"""
  id: String
  """IDs of artifacts this definition produces or consumes"""
  artifactIds: [String!]
  """How to invoke the execution"""
  executor: ExecutorSpec
  """How to interpret the result"""
  result: ResultSpec
  """Evaluation criteria and thresholds"""
  evaluation: Evaluation
  """Whether the definition is currently active"""
  active: Boolean
  """Whether the definition must pass for merge"""
  required: Boolean
  """Whether this definition was discovered from the docgraph"""
  graphSource: Boolean
  """When the definition was created"""
  createdAt: DateTime
}

"""ExecutorSpec describes how to invoke an execution"""
type ExecutorSpec {
  """Execution kind (e.g. "shell", "agent")"""
  kind: String!
  """Command and arguments to run"""
  command: [String!]
  """Working directory for the command"""
  cwd: String
  """Environment variables as key-value pairs"""
  env: JSON
  """Maximum execution time in milliseconds"""
  timeoutMs: Int
}

"""ResultSpec describes how to interpret an execution result"""
type ResultSpec {
  """Metric-shaped projection (null if not metric-typed)"""
  metric: MetricResultSpec
}

"""MetricResultSpec defines metric extraction from execution output"""
type MetricResultSpec {
  """Unit of measurement (e.g. "ms", "bytes")"""
  unit: String
  """JSONPath to the numeric value"""
  valuePath: String
  """JSONPath to the samples array"""
  samplesPath: String
}

"""Evaluation holds interpretation rules for execution results"""
type Evaluation {
  """Comparison operator (e.g. "lt", "gt", "eq")"""
  comparison: String
  """Threshold values for warn/ratchet"""
  thresholds: Thresholds
}

"""Thresholds captures the ratchet policy for metric evaluations"""
type Thresholds {
  """Warn threshold in milliseconds"""
  warnMs: Float
  """Ratchet threshold in milliseconds"""
  ratchetMs: Float
}

"""DocumentEdge is one edge in a DocumentConnection"""

# ============================================
# Workers
# ============================================

"""Worker represents an execute-loop worker process"""
type Worker implements Node {
  """Unique worker identifier"""
  id: ID!
  """Worker kind (e.g. "execute-loop")"""
  kind: String!
  """Current lifecycle state (e.g. "running", "idle", "stopped")"""
  state: String!
  """Human-readable status summary"""
  status: String
  """Absolute path to the project this worker operates on"""
  projectRoot: String!
  """Agent harness name (e.g. "claude", "codex")"""
  harness: String
  """Provider/backend name"""
  provider: String
  """Model identifier used for execution"""
  model: String
  """Effort level (e.g. "low", "medium", "high")"""
  effort: String
  """True if the worker should stop after one bead"""
  once: Boolean
  """Interval between poll cycles (e.g. "30s")"""
  pollInterval: String
  """When the worker was started"""
  startedAt: DateTime
  """When the worker finished (null if still running)"""
  finishedAt: DateTime
  """Fatal error that stopped the worker"""
  error: String
  """Path to the worker's stdout log file"""
  stdoutPath: String
  """Path to the worker's spec/config file"""
  specPath: String
  """Total number of execution attempts"""
  attempts: Int
  """Number of successful executions"""
  successes: Int
  """Number of failed executions"""
  failures: Int
  """Bead ID currently being executed (null if idle)"""
  currentBead: String
  """Most recent error message from an execution"""
  lastError: String
  """Result of the most recently completed execution"""
  lastResult: WorkerExecutionResult
  """In-flight attempt summary (null if idle)"""
  currentAttempt: CurrentAttemptInfo
  """Recent phase transitions for observability"""
  recentPhases: [PhaseTransition!]
  """Summary of the most recently completed attempt"""
  lastAttempt: LastAttemptInfo
  """Land coordinator metrics for this worker's project"""
  landSummary: CoordinatorMetrics
}

"""CurrentAttemptInfo summarises the in-flight execution attempt"""
type CurrentAttemptInfo {
  """Unique attempt identifier"""
  attemptId: String!
  """Bead being executed"""
  beadId: String!
  """Title of the bead being executed"""
  beadTitle: String
  """Harness running this attempt"""
  harness: String
  """Model used for this attempt"""
  model: String
  """Profile/configuration name"""
  profile: String
  """Current execution phase"""
  phase: String!
  """Sequence number of the current phase"""
  phaseSeq: Int!
  """When this attempt started"""
  startedAt: DateTime!
  """Milliseconds elapsed since attempt start"""
  elapsedMs: Int!
}

"""PhaseTransition records a single phase change event"""
type PhaseTransition {
  """Phase name entered"""
  phase: String!
  """Timestamp of the transition"""
  ts: DateTime!
  """Sequence number of this phase"""
  phaseSeq: Int!
}

"""LastAttemptInfo summarises the most recently completed attempt"""
type LastAttemptInfo {
  """Unique attempt identifier"""
  attemptId: String!
  """Bead that was executed"""
  beadId: String!
  """Final phase reached"""
  phase: String!
  """When the attempt started"""
  startedAt: DateTime!
  """When the attempt ended"""
  endedAt: DateTime!
  """Total duration in milliseconds"""
  elapsedMs: Int!
}

"""WorkerExecutionResult captures the outcome of one execution"""
type WorkerExecutionResult {
  """Bead that was executed"""
  beadId: String
  """Unique attempt identifier"""
  attemptId: String
  """Worker that ran the execution"""
  workerId: String
  """Harness used"""
  harness: String
  """Provider used"""
  provider: String
  """Model used"""
  model: String
  """Outcome status (e.g. "success", "failure", "timeout")"""
  status: String
  """Human-readable outcome detail"""
  detail: String
  """Correlated agent session ID"""
  sessionId: String
  """Git revision the execution started from"""
  baseRev: String
  """Git revision produced by the execution"""
  resultRev: String
  """Suggested retry delay (e.g. "30s")"""
  retryAfter: String
}

"""WorkerEdge is one edge in a WorkerConnection"""
type WorkerEdge {
  """The worker at this position"""
  node: Worker!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""WorkerConnection is a Relay cursor connection of workers"""
type WorkerConnection {
  """Ordered list of worker edges"""
  edges: [WorkerEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of workers matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

"""WorkerLog holds captured output from a worker process"""
type WorkerLog {
  """Captured standard output"""
  stdout: String!
  """Captured standard error"""
  stderr: String!
}

  """Number of beads that have been reopened"""

  """Beads with unknown cycle time data"""
  unknownCycleTime: Int!
  """Beads with known cost data"""
  knownCost: Int!
  """Beads with estimated cost data"""

  """Beads with unknown cost data"""
  unknownCost: Int!
}

  """Sessions not correlated to any bead"""

  """Total prompt/input tokens consumed"""
  inputTokens: Int!
  """Total completion/output tokens generated"""
  outputTokens: Int!
  """Total tokens (input + output)"""
  totalTokens: Int!
  """Sessions with known cost data"""
  knownCost: Int!
  """Sessions with estimated cost data"""
  estimatedCost: Int!
  """Sessions with unknown cost data"""
  unknownCost: Int!
  """Total cost in USD"""

- Verify after editing when builds or tests exist
- Stay concise
- Don't add unrequested features
- Fix errors rather than reporting them

# DDx Agent Instructions

This repository uses DDx's built-in bead tracker for durable work management.

## Bead Policy

- Treat `.ddx/beads.jsonl` as DDx-managed data, not as a hand-edited document.
- Create beads only with `ddx bead create`.
- Modify bead metadata only with `ddx bead update`.
- Manage dependencies only with `ddx bead dep add` and `ddx bead dep remove`.
- Close work only with `ddx bead close`.
- Use `ddx bead import` and `ddx bead export` for bulk migration or interchange.
- Commit tracker mutations by default after bead commands.
- If the tracker change stands alone, make a tracker-only commit promptly.
- If it belongs to related implementation/docs work already being prepared for
  commit, fold the tracker change into that same commit instead of leaving
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` dirty.

## Prohibited Actions

- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.
- Do not add, remove, or rewrite bead rows with `apply_patch`, editors, scripts, or ad hoc JSONL manipulation.
- Do not invent bead IDs or prefixes such as `hx-*` or `ddx-*`.
- Do not treat nearby tracker entries as a naming pattern to copy.

## If The CLI Seems Insufficient

- Prefer the nearest supported `ddx bead` command.
- If the required tracker operation is not supported by `ddx bead`, stop and ask rather than editing tracker storage directly.

## Verification

- Use `ddx bead show <id>` to inspect one bead.
- Use `ddx bead list`, `ddx bead ready`, and `ddx bead status` to inspect queue state.
- Use `ddx bead --help` and `ddx bead create --help` before assuming a flag exists.

## Skill Policy

- Treat `SKILL.md` frontmatter as a strict interface, not freeform metadata.
- Published DDx skills must use top-level YAML frontmatter fields `name` and `description`.
- Use `argument-hint` only when the skill accepts a trailing positional or shorthand invocation hint.
- Do not use nested `skill:` frontmatter for DDx repo skills.
- Run `ddx skills check [path ...]` for reusable validation across repo skills and plugin skills.
- Run `make skill-schema` after editing any file under `skills/*/SKILL.md` or `cli/internal/skills/*/SKILL.md`.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.

DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output

## Architecture

This monorepo produces three artifacts:
- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site

### Key Components

- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
  - `main.go` - Application entry point
- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`)
  - `templates/` - Project templates
  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns
  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions
  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions
  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry
- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)
- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts

## Development Commands

### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)

```bash
# Build and test
make build          # Build for current platform
make test           # Run Go tests
make lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)
make fmt            # Format Go code

# Development workflow
make all            # Clean, deps, test, build
make dev            # Development mode with file watching (requires air)
make run ARGS="..."  # Run CLI with arguments
make install        # Install locally to ~/.local/bin/ddx

# Dependencies
make deps           # Install and tidy Go modules
make update-deps    # Update all dependencies

# Multi-platform builds
make build-all      # Build for all platforms
make release        # Create release archives
```

### Frontend Development (SvelteKit)

The web UI is a SvelteKit application built with Bun. Run from the repository root:

```bash
# Install frontend dependencies and start dev server
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun install && bun run dev

# Generate GraphQL types from schema
bun run houdini:generate

# Run unit tests
bun run test

# Run e2e tests with Playwright
bun run test:e2e
```

Frontend build output is embedded into the Go binary via `//go:embed`.

### Project Structure Navigation

The CLI uses git subtree for managing the relationship between individual projects and the master DDx repository. The `.ddx.yml` configuration file defines:
- Repository URL and branch
- Included resources (prompts, scripts, templates, patterns)
- Template variables and overrides
- Git subtree settings

### Key Patterns

1. **Command Structure**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate file in `cli/cmd/`
2. **Configuration Management**: Uses Viper for config file handling with YAML format
3. **Template Processing**: Variable substitution system for customizing templates
4. **Git Integration**: Built on git subtree for reliable version control and contribution workflows
5. **Cross-Platform Support**: Makefile supports building for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)

## Architectural Principles

**CRITICAL**: The DDx CLI follows the principle of "Extensibility Through Composition" - keep the CLI core minimal and add features through library resources.

1. **Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows**:
   - CLI owns: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
   - Workflow tools own: phase enforcement, supervisory loops, methodology-specific validation
   - DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions

2. **Feature Addition Pattern**:
   - Document-type resources go in the library (personas, patterns, templates)
   - Platform capabilities go in the CLI (beads, agent service, MCP)
   - Workflow-specific logic stays in workflow tools (HELIX, etc.)

## Testing and Quality

**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:

```bash
# Run full test suite - all tests must pass
cd cli && go test -v ./cmd
```

Every test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.

- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code
- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)
- Code formatting with `go fmt`
- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained

### Pre-commit Checks

The project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:

```bash
# Run all pre-commit checks
lefthook run pre-commit

# Or stage files and run checks
git add <files>
lefthook run pre-commit
```

Pre-commit checks include:
- Secrets detection
- Binary file prevention
- Debug statement detection
- Merge conflict detection
- DDx configuration validation
- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing

## CLI Command Overview

The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:

**Core Commands:**
- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project
- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues
- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version
- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository
- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community
- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status

**Bead Tracker:**
- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD
- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads
- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management
- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br

**Agent Service:**
- `ddx agent run --harness=<name> --prompt <file>` - Invoke an AI agent
- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus
- `ddx agent execute-bead <id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result
- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses
- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check
- `ddx agent log` - Session history

**Resource Commands:**
- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts
- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates
- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas
- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers

**Embedded Utilities:**
- `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required

## Persona System

DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:

- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `strict-code-reviewer`, `test-engineer-tdd`)
- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`

Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.

# System Instructions

**Execute ONLY what is requested:**

- **YAGNI** (You Aren't Gonna Need It): Implement only specified features. No "useful additions" or "while we're here" features.
- **KISS** (Keep It Simple, Stupid): Choose the simplest solution that meets requirements. Avoid clever code or premature optimization.
- **DOWITYTD** (Do Only What I Told You To Do): Stop when the task is complete. No extra refactoring, documentation, or improvements unless explicitly requested.

**Response Style:**
- Be concise and direct
- Skip preamble and postamble
- Provide complete information without unnecessary elaboration
- Stop immediately when the task is done

**When coding:**
- Write only code needed to pass tests
- No gold-plating or speculative features
- Follow existing patterns and conventions
- Add only requested functionality

NEVER proactively create documentation files (*.md) or README files unless the task explicitly requires creating or editing documentation.

<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-0b9c980a">
    <title>exec: execute-loop --project flag to target a specific project when server is multi-project</title>
    <description>
'ddx agent execute-loop' help says 'execute-loop scans the current project's execution-ready bead queue.' When submitting to a multi-project server, there is no way to say 'run this loop against project X'. The server picks whichever project is the current server context, ignoring the caller's CWD.

## Observed

From CWD /Users/erik/Projects/axon:

  $ ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --json
  {
    "project_root": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx",  # WRONG
    "state": "running",
    "worker_id": "worker-20260415T011331-d0a4"
  }

I wanted the worker to pick an axon bead. The server ran the worker against the ddx repo instead. There is no --project flag, no env var, nothing in the CLI to redirect the server's project scope.

Currently the only way to target a specific project with execute-loop is --local mode (which spawns a subprocess using the caller's CWD as the project root). This defeats the purpose of having a server in a multi-project deployment.

## Scope

- --project <path> or --project <name> flag on execute-loop, execute-bead, and 'ddx agent run' that tells the server which project_root to use
- Env var fallback: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT or similar
- Default to CWD if unset and no flag
- Server refuses ambiguous/unmatched project names with a clean error
- Integration test: from CWD A, submit a loop targeting project B; assert worker runs in B's project_root

## Why this matters for the user's workflow

Without this, the ONLY way to drive multi-project execution from one shell is --local, which (a) loses the server's queue visibility, (b) ties the parent process to the child, and (c) negates the 'ddx agent execute-loop submits to server by default' ergonomic that execute-loop's --help promises.

## Acceptance

- [ ] --project flag wired through execute-loop/execute-bead/run
- [ ] Env var fallback documented
- [ ] Default behavior: CWD-based project detection
- [ ] Integration test for cross-project submission
- [ ] execute-loop --help updated to describe project targeting

  <instructions>
You are a coding agent executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. You have tools: read, write, edit, bash, ls, grep, find. Use them directly — do not shell out to cat/tail/rg/find; use the tools.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract. Every AC checkbox must be satisfied by code you write in this pass.

## Process

1. **Read first.** Before writing any code, read the files the bead description names. If the description says "A1 landed X at <path>", read that path. If it references a spec section, read that section. Do NOT start editing without seeing the existing code — this is how you avoid making the same change twice or breaking an invariant you did not know about.

2. **Plan the work briefly.** Internally note which files you will touch, which tests you will add, and which verification commands you will run. Keep the plan short — one or two sentences.

3. **Implement.** Use `edit` for targeted changes to existing files and `write` for brand-new files. Use `read` (not `bash: cat`) to read files. Use `grep` (not `bash: rg`) to search. Use `ls` (not `bash: ls`) for directory listings.

4. **Verify before committing.** Run the project's test and lint commands — typically `cargo test -p <crate>` and `cargo clippy -p <crate> -- -D warnings` for Rust, `bun test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description will usually name the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If anything fails, diagnose and fix it before committing.

5. **Commit exactly once.** Use `git add <specific-paths>` with the file paths you actually changed — never `git add -A`, which can pick up unrelated files in the worktree. Use a conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Commit implementation and tests in the same commit so the reviewer sees them together.

6. **Stop after the commit succeeds.** Return immediately. Do not continue reading files, running extra tests, or asking yourself follow-up questions — the work is done, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success to the orchestrator. Continuing past the commit risks runaway loops.

## If you cannot finish

Write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260415T053924-05dbebde/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up would need. Do NOT commit partial or red code — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, every BLOCKING finding in it is a concrete thing the previous attempt missed. Address each one before declaring the work complete — do not declare no_changes with blocking findings still unaddressed.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new files, write them. Conservative rules (YAGNI, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not touch CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.
- Stage only the files you intentionally changed.

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestAgentExecuteLoopUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan(t *testing.T) {
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	subdir := filepath.Join(env.Dir, "nested", "path")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(subdir, 0o755))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(subdir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()

	out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-loop", "--local", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
		NoReadyWork bool   `json:"no_ready_work"`
		Attempts    int    `json:"attempts"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.Equal(t, env.Dir, res.ProjectRoot)
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, res.Attempts)
}

func TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()
	// Init git repo so HEAD can be resolved
	out, err := exec.Command("git", "init", workDir).CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	// Create an initial commit so HEAD exists
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "README.md"), []byte("# test"), 0o644))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", "-A").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))

	seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
		ID:     "my-bead",
		Title:  "Test bead",
		Status: bead.StatusOpen,
	})
	// Commit the beads.jsonl so it's in the worktree snapshot
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "add beads").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))

	git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
		mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
		wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111",
		dirty:       true,
	}
	runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}

	res, err := agent.ExecuteBead(workDir, "my-bead", agent.ExecuteBeadOptions{}, git, runner)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
	assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, res.Status)
}

# DDx GraphQL Schema
# Single source of truth for the SvelteKit UI.
# Every type field is doc-commented. Relay cursor connections on all lists.

# ============================================
# Scalars
# ============================================

"""ISO-8601 datetime string"""
scalar DateTime

"""Arbitrary JSON blob (used for opaque map fields)"""
scalar JSON

# ============================================
# Interfaces
# ============================================

"""Node is the Relay global object identification interface"""
interface Node {
  """Globally unique identifier"""
  id: ID!
}

# ============================================
# Pagination (Relay Cursor Connections)
# ============================================

"""PageInfo carries cursor-based pagination state (Relay spec)"""
type PageInfo {
  """True when more edges exist after the last returned edge"""
  hasNextPage: Boolean!
  """True when more edges exist before the first returned edge"""
  hasPreviousPage: Boolean!
  """Cursor of the first edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  startCursor: String
  """Cursor of the last edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  endCursor: String
}

# ============================================
# Node & Project
# ============================================

"""NodeInfo represents the running ddx-server instance"""
type NodeInfo implements Node {
  """Unique node identifier (stable across restarts)"""
  id: ID!
  """Human-readable node name"""
  name: String!
  """Timestamp when the server process started"""
  startedAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the most recent heartbeat"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
}

"""Project represents a registered ddx project on this node"""
type Project implements Node {
  """Unique project identifier"""
  id: ID!
  """Short display name"""
  name: String!
  """Absolute filesystem path to the project root"""
  path: String!
  """Git remote URL, if configured"""
  gitRemote: String
  """When the project was first registered with the server"""
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the last successful probe"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
  """True when the project path is inaccessible"""
  unreachable: Boolean
  """Set when the project has been tombstoned (soft-deleted)"""
  tombstonedAt: DateTime
}

"""ProjectEdge is one edge in a ProjectConnection"""
type ProjectEdge {
  """The project at this position"""
  node: Project!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""ProjectConnection is a Relay cursor connection of projects"""
type ProjectConnection {
  """Ordered list of project edges"""
  edges: [ProjectEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of projects matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Beads
# ============================================

"""Bead is a portable work item with lifecycle metadata"""
type Bead implements Node {
  """Unique bead identifier (e.g. "ddx-a1b2c3d4")"""
  id: ID!
  """Short summary of the work"""
  title: String!
  """Lifecycle status: open, in-progress, closed, blocked"""
  status: String!
  """Numeric priority (lower is higher priority)"""
  priority: Int!
  """Issue classification (e.g. "task", "bug", "spike")"""
  issueType: String!
  """Agent or human currently responsible"""
  owner: String
  """When the bead was created"""
  createdAt: DateTime!
  """Who or what created the bead"""
  createdBy: String
  """Last modification timestamp"""
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  """Freeform labels for filtering and grouping"""
  labels: [String!]
  """Parent bead ID for hierarchy"""
  parent: String
  """Full description / body text"""
  description: String
  """Acceptance criteria text"""
  acceptance: String
  """Freeform notes appended during work"""
  notes: String
  """Dependency edges to other beads"""
  dependencies: [Dependency!]
}

"""Dependency is a directed edge between two beads"""
type Dependency {
  """The bead that has the dependency"""
  issueId: String!
  """The bead that must be completed first"""
  dependsOnId: String!
  """Dependency kind (e.g. "blocks", "relates-to")"""
  type: String!
  """When the dependency was created"""
  createdAt: String
  """Who created the dependency"""
  createdBy: String
  """Opaque metadata attached to the link"""
  metadata: String
}

"""BeadEdge is one edge in a BeadConnection"""
type BeadEdge {
  """The bead at this position"""
  node: Bead!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""BeadConnection is a Relay cursor connection of beads"""
type BeadConnection {
  """Ordered list of bead edges"""
  edges: [BeadEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of beads matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

"""BeadStatusCounts holds aggregate bead counts by status"""
type BeadStatusCounts {
  """Number of beads in open status"""
  open: Int!
  """Number of beads in closed status"""
  closed: Int!
  """Number of beads with unclosed dependencies"""
  blocked: Int!
  """Number of open beads whose dependencies are all closed"""
  ready: Int!
  """Total number of beads"""
  total: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Documents (library)
# ============================================

"""Document represents a docgraph document with dependency tracking"""
type Document implements Node {
  """Document identifier (usually the relative path)"""
  id: ID!
  """Filesystem path relative to the library root"""
  path: String!
  """Human-readable title extracted from frontmatter"""
  title: String!
  """IDs of documents this document depends on"""
  dependsOn: [String!]!
  """IDs of input artifacts consumed by this document"""
  inputs: [String!]!
  """IDs of documents that depend on this document"""
  dependents: [String!]!
  """Review state metadata (null if never reviewed)"""
  review: DocumentReview
  """True if parked (excluded from staleness checks)"""
  parkingLot: Boolean!
  """Prompt text embedded in the document frontmatter"""
  prompt: String
  """Execution definition embedded in the document (null if none)"""
  execDef: DocumentExecDef
}

"""DocumentReview holds review-state metadata for a document"""
type DocumentReview {
  """Hash of the document content at review time"""
  selfHash: String!
  """Map of dependency IDs to their hashes at review time"""
  deps: JSON!
  """ISO-8601 timestamp of the last review"""
  reviewedAt: String!
}

"""DocumentExecDef holds an execution definition embedded in a document"""
type DocumentExecDef {
  """Definition identifier"""
  id: String
  """IDs of artifacts this definition produces or consumes"""
  artifactIds: [String!]
  """How to invoke the execution"""
  executor: ExecutorSpec
  """How to interpret the result"""
  result: ResultSpec
  """Evaluation criteria and thresholds"""
  evaluation: Evaluation
  """Whether the definition is currently active"""
  active: Boolean
  """Whether the definition must pass for merge"""
  required: Boolean
  """Whether this definition was discovered from the docgraph"""
  graphSource: Boolean
  """When the definition was created"""
  createdAt: DateTime
}

"""ExecutorSpec describes how to invoke an execution"""
type ExecutorSpec {
  """Execution kind (e.g. "shell", "agent")"""
  kind: String!
  """Command and arguments to run"""
  command: [String!]
  """Working directory for the command"""
  cwd: String
  """Environment variables as key-value pairs"""
  env: JSON
  """Maximum execution time in milliseconds"""
  timeoutMs: Int
}

"""ResultSpec describes how to interpret an execution result"""
type ResultSpec {
  """Metric-shaped projection (null if not metric-typed)"""
  metric: MetricResultSpec
}

"""MetricResultSpec defines metric extraction from execution output"""
type MetricResultSpec {
  """Unit of measurement (e.g. "ms", "bytes")"""
  unit: String
  """JSONPath to the numeric value"""
  valuePath: String
  """JSONPath to the samples array"""
  samplesPath: String
}

"""Evaluation holds interpretation rules for execution results"""
type Evaluation {
  """Comparison operator (e.g. "lt", "gt", "eq")"""
  comparison: String
  """Threshold values for warn/ratchet"""
  thresholds: Thresholds
}

"""Thresholds captures the ratchet policy for metric evaluations"""

"""Worker represents an execute-loop worker process"""

  """Number of failed executions"""

  """Human-readable outcome detail"""

  """Git revision produced by the execution"""

# DDx Agent Instructions

This repository uses DDx's built-in bead tracker for durable work management.

## Bead Policy

- Treat `.ddx/beads.jsonl` as DDx-managed data, not as a hand-edited document.
- Create beads only with `ddx bead create`.
- Modify bead metadata only with `ddx bead update`.
- Manage dependencies only with `ddx bead dep add` and `ddx bead dep remove`.
- Close work only with `ddx bead close`.
- Use `ddx bead import` and `ddx bead export` for bulk migration or interchange.
- Commit tracker mutations by default after bead commands.
- If the tracker change stands alone, make a tracker-only commit promptly.
- If it belongs to related implementation/docs work already being prepared for
  commit, fold the tracker change into that same commit instead of leaving
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` dirty.

## Prohibited Actions

- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.
- Do not add, remove, or rewrite bead rows with `apply_patch`, editors, scripts, or ad hoc JSONL manipulation.
- Do not invent bead IDs or prefixes such as `hx-*` or `ddx-*`.
- Do not treat nearby tracker entries as a naming pattern to copy.

## If The CLI Seems Insufficient

- Prefer the nearest supported `ddx bead` command.
- If the required tracker operation is not supported by `ddx bead`, stop and ask rather than editing tracker storage directly.

## Verification

- Use `ddx bead show <id>` to inspect one bead.
- Use `ddx bead list`, `ddx bead ready`, and `ddx bead status` to inspect queue state.
- Use `ddx bead --help` and `ddx bead create --help` before assuming a flag exists.

## Skill Policy

- Treat `SKILL.md` frontmatter as a strict interface, not freeform metadata.
- Published DDx skills must use top-level YAML frontmatter fields `name` and `description`.
- Use `argument-hint` only when the skill accepts a trailing positional or shorthand invocation hint.
- Do not use nested `skill:` frontmatter for DDx repo skills.
- Run `ddx skills check [path ...]` for reusable validation across repo skills and plugin skills.
- Run `make skill-schema` after editing any file under `skills/*/SKILL.md` or `cli/internal/skills/*/SKILL.md`.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.

DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output

## Architecture

This monorepo produces three artifacts:
- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site

### Key Components

- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
  - `main.go` - Application entry point
- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`)
  - `templates/` - Project templates
  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns
  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions
  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions
  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry
- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)
- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts

## Development Commands

### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)

```bash
# Build and test
make build          # Build for current platform
make test           # Run Go tests
make lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)
make fmt            # Format Go code

# Development workflow
make all            # Clean, deps, test, build
make dev            # Development mode with file watching (requires air)
make run ARGS="..."  # Run CLI with arguments
make install        # Install locally to ~/.local/bin/ddx

# Dependencies
make deps           # Install and tidy Go modules
make update-deps    # Update all dependencies

# Multi-platform builds
make build-all      # Build for all platforms
make release        # Create release archives
```

### Frontend Development (SvelteKit)

The web UI is a SvelteKit application built with Bun. Run from the repository root:

```bash
# Install frontend dependencies and start dev server
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun install && bun run dev

# Generate GraphQL types from schema
bun run houdini:generate

# Run unit tests
bun run test

# Run e2e tests with Playwright
bun run test:e2e
```

Frontend build output is embedded into the Go binary via `//go:embed`.

### Project Structure Navigation

The CLI uses git subtree for managing the relationship between individual projects and the master DDx repository. The `.ddx.yml` configuration file defines:
- Repository URL and branch
- Included resources (prompts, scripts, templates, patterns)
- Template variables and overrides
- Git subtree settings

### Key Patterns

1. **Command Structure**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate file in `cli/cmd/`
2. **Configuration Management**: Uses Viper for config file handling with YAML format
3. **Template Processing**: Variable substitution system for customizing templates
4. **Git Integration**: Built on git subtree for reliable version control and contribution workflows
5. **Cross-Platform Support**: Makefile supports building for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)

## Architectural Principles

**CRITICAL**: The DDx CLI follows the principle of "Extensibility Through Composition" - keep the CLI core minimal and add features through library resources.

1. **Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows**:
   - CLI owns: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
   - Workflow tools own: phase enforcement, supervisory loops, methodology-specific validation
   - DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions

2. **Feature Addition Pattern**:
   - Document-type resources go in the library (personas, patterns, templates)
   - Platform capabilities go in the CLI (beads, agent service, MCP)
   - Workflow-specific logic stays in workflow tools (HELIX, etc.)

## Testing and Quality

**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:

```bash
# Run full test suite - all tests must pass
cd cli && go test -v ./cmd
```

Every test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.

- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code
- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)
- Code formatting with `go fmt`
- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained

### Pre-commit Checks

The project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:

```bash
# Run all pre-commit checks
lefthook run pre-commit

# Or stage files and run checks
git add <files>
lefthook run pre-commit
```

Pre-commit checks include:
- Secrets detection
- Binary file prevention
- Debug statement detection
- Merge conflict detection
- DDx configuration validation
- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing

## CLI Command Overview

The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:

**Core Commands:**
- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project
- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues
- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version
- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository
- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community
- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status

**Bead Tracker:**
- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD
- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads
- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management
- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br

**Agent Service:**
- `ddx agent run --harness=<name> --prompt <file>` - Invoke an AI agent
- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus
- `ddx agent execute-bead <id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result
- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses
- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check
- `ddx agent log` - Session history

**Resource Commands:**
- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts
- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates
- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas
- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers

**Embedded Utilities:**
- `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required

## Persona System

DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:

- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `strict-code-reviewer`, `test-engineer-tdd`)
- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`

Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.

# System Instructions

**Execute ONLY what is requested:**

- **YAGNI** (You Aren't Gonna Need It): Implement only specified features. No "useful additions" or "while we're here" features.
- **KISS** (Keep It Simple, Stupid): Choose the simplest solution that meets requirements. Avoid clever code or premature optimization.
- **DOWITYTD** (Do Only What I Told You To Do): Stop when the task is complete. No extra refactoring, documentation, or improvements unless explicitly requested.

**Response Style:**
- Be concise and direct
- Skip preamble and postamble
- Provide complete information without unnecessary elaboration
- Stop immediately when the task is done

**When coding:**
- Write only code needed to pass tests
- No gold-plating or speculative features
- Follow existing patterns and conventions
- Add only requested functionality

NEVER proactively create documentation files (*.md) or README files unless the task explicitly requires creating or editing documentation.

<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-0b9c980a">
    <title>exec: execute-loop --project flag to target a specific project when server is multi-project</title>
    <description>
'ddx agent execute-loop' help says 'execute-loop scans the current project's execution-ready bead queue.' When submitting to a multi-project server, there is no way to say 'run this loop against project X'. The server picks whichever project is the current server context, ignoring the caller's CWD.

## Observed

From CWD /Users/erik/Projects/axon:

  $ ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --json
  {
    "project_root": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx",  # WRONG
    "state": "running",
    "worker_id": "worker-20260415T011331-d0a4"
  }

I wanted the worker to pick an axon bead. The server ran the worker against the ddx repo instead. There is no --project flag, no env var, nothing in the CLI to redirect the server's project scope.

Currently the only way to target a specific project with execute-loop is --local mode (which spawns a subprocess using the caller's CWD as the project root). This defeats the purpose of having a server in a multi-project deployment.

## Scope

- --project <path> or --project <name> flag on execute-loop, execute-bead, and 'ddx agent run' that tells the server which project_root to use
- Env var fallback: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT or similar
- Default to CWD if unset and no flag
- Server refuses ambiguous/unmatched project names with a clean error
- Integration test: from CWD A, submit a loop targeting project B; assert worker runs in B's project_root

## Why this matters for the user's workflow

Without this, the ONLY way to drive multi-project execution from one shell is --local, which (a) loses the server's queue visibility, (b) ties the parent process to the child, and (c) negates the 'ddx agent execute-loop submits to server by default' ergonomic that execute-loop's --help promises.

## Acceptance

- [ ] --project flag wired through execute-loop/execute-bead/run
- [ ] Env var fallback documented
- [ ] Default behavior: CWD-based project detection
- [ ] Integration test for cross-project submission
- [ ] execute-loop --help updated to describe project targeting

  <instructions>
You are a coding agent executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. You have tools: read, write, edit, bash, ls, grep, find. Use them directly — do not shell out to cat/tail/rg/find; use the tools.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract. Every AC checkbox must be satisfied by code you write in this pass.

## Process

1. **Read first.** Before writing any code, read the files the bead description names. If the description says "A1 landed X at <path>", read that path. If it references a spec section, read that section. Do NOT start editing without seeing the existing code — this is how you avoid making the same change twice or breaking an invariant you did not know about.

2. **Plan the work briefly.** Internally note which files you will touch, which tests you will add, and which verification commands you will run. Keep the plan short — one or two sentences.

3. **Implement.** Use `edit` for targeted changes to existing files and `write` for brand-new files. Use `read` (not `bash: cat`) to read files. Use `grep` (not `bash: rg`) to search. Use `ls` (not `bash: ls`) for directory listings.

4. **Verify before committing.** Run the project's test and lint commands — typically `cargo test -p <crate>` and `cargo clippy -p <crate> -- -D warnings` for Rust, `bun test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description will usually name the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If anything fails, diagnose and fix it before committing.

5. **Commit exactly once.** Use `git add <specific-paths>` with the file paths you actually changed — never `git add -A`, which can pick up unrelated files in the worktree. Use a conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Commit implementation and tests in the same commit so the reviewer sees them together.

6. **Stop after the commit succeeds.** Return immediately. Do not continue reading files, running extra tests, or asking yourself follow-up questions — the work is done, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success to the orchestrator. Continuing past the commit risks runaway loops.

## If you cannot finish

Write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260415T053924-05dbebde/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up would need. Do NOT commit partial or red code — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, every BLOCKING finding in it is a concrete thing the previous attempt missed. Address each one before declaring the work complete — do not declare no_changes with blocking findings still unaddressed.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new files, write them. Conservative rules (YAGNI, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not touch CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.
- Stage only the files you intentionally changed.

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestAgentExecuteLoopUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan(t *testing.T) {
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	subdir := filepath.Join(env.Dir, "nested", "path")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(subdir, 0o755))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(subdir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()

	out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-loop", "--local", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
		NoReadyWork bool   `json:"no_ready_work"`
		Attempts    int    `json:"attempts"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.Equal(t, env.Dir, res.ProjectRoot)
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, res.Attempts)
}

func TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()
	// Init git repo so HEAD can be resolved
	out, err := exec.Command("git", "init", workDir).CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	// Create an initial commit so HEAD exists
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "README.md"), []byte("# test"), 0o644))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", "-A").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))

	seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
		ID:     "my-bead",
		Title:  "Test bead",
		Status: bead.StatusOpen,
	})
	// Commit the beads.jsonl so it's in the worktree snapshot
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "add beads").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))

	git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
		mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
		wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111",
		dirty:       true,
	}
	runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}

	res, err := agent.ExecuteBead(workDir, "my-bead", agent.ExecuteBeadOptions{}, git, runner)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
	assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, res.Status)
}

# DDx GraphQL Schema
# Single source of truth for the SvelteKit UI.
# Every type field is doc-commented. Relay cursor connections on all lists.

# ============================================
# Scalars
# ============================================

"""ISO-8601 datetime string"""
scalar DateTime

"""Arbitrary JSON blob (used for opaque map fields)"""
scalar JSON

# ============================================
# Interfaces
# ============================================

"""Node is the Relay global object identification interface"""
interface Node {
  """Globally unique identifier"""
  id: ID!
}

# ============================================
# Pagination (Relay Cursor Connections)
# ============================================

"""PageInfo carries cursor-based pagination state (Relay spec)"""
type PageInfo {
  """True when more edges exist after the last returned edge"""
  hasNextPage: Boolean!
  """True when more edges exist before the first returned edge"""
  hasPreviousPage: Boolean!
  """Cursor of the first edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  startCursor: String
  """Cursor of the last edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  endCursor: String
}

# ============================================
# Node & Project
# ============================================

"""NodeInfo represents the running ddx-server instance"""
type NodeInfo implements Node {
  """Unique node identifier (stable across restarts)"""
  id: ID!
  """Human-readable node name"""
  name: String!
  """Timestamp when the server process started"""
  startedAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the most recent heartbeat"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
}

"""Project represents a registered ddx project on this node"""
type Project implements Node {
  """Unique project identifier"""
  id: ID!
  """Short display name"""
  name: String!
  """Absolute filesystem path to the project root"""
  path: String!
  """Git remote URL, if configured"""
  gitRemote: String
  """When the project was first registered with the server"""
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the last successful probe"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
  """True when the project path is inaccessible"""
  unreachable: Boolean
  """Set when the project has been tombstoned (soft-deleted)"""
  tombstonedAt: DateTime
}

"""ProjectEdge is one edge in a ProjectConnection"""
type ProjectEdge {
  """The project at this position"""
  node: Project!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""ProjectConnection is a Relay cursor connection of projects"""
type ProjectConnection {
  """Ordered list of project edges"""
  edges: [ProjectEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of projects matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Beads
# ============================================

"""Bead is a portable work item with lifecycle metadata"""
type Bead implements Node {
  """Unique bead identifier (e.g. "ddx-a1b2c3d4")"""
  id: ID!
  """Short summary of the work"""
  title: String!
  """Lifecycle status: open, in-progress, closed, blocked"""
  status: String!
  """Numeric priority (lower is higher priority)"""
  priority: Int!
  """Issue classification (e.g. "task", "bug", "spike")"""
  issueType: String!
  """Agent or human currently responsible"""
  owner: String
  """When the bead was created"""
  createdAt: DateTime!
  """Who or what created the bead"""
  createdBy: String
  """Last modification timestamp"""
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  """Freeform labels for filtering and grouping"""
  labels: [String!]
  """Parent bead ID for hierarchy"""
  parent: String
  """Full description / body text"""
  description: String
  """Acceptance criteria text"""
  acceptance: String
  """Freeform notes appended during work"""
  notes: String
  """Dependency edges to other beads"""
  dependencies: [Dependency!]
}

"""Dependency is a directed edge between two beads"""
type Dependency {
  """The bead that has the dependency"""
  issueId: String!
  """The bead that must be completed first"""
  dependsOnId: String!
  """Dependency kind (e.g. "blocks", "relates-to")"""
  type: String!
  """When the dependency was created"""
  createdAt: String
  """Who created the dependency"""
  createdBy: String
  """Opaque metadata attached to the link"""
  metadata: String
}

"""BeadEdge is one edge in a BeadConnection"""
type BeadEdge {
  """The bead at this position"""
  node: Bead!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""BeadConnection is a Relay cursor connection of beads"""
type BeadConnection {
  """Ordered list of bead edges"""
  edges: [BeadEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of beads matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

"""BeadStatusCounts holds aggregate bead counts by status"""
type BeadStatusCounts {
  """Number of beads in open status"""
  open: Int!
  """Number of beads in closed status"""
  closed: Int!
  """Number of beads with unclosed dependencies"""
  blocked: Int!
  """Number of open beads whose dependencies are all closed"""
  ready: Int!
  """Total number of beads"""
  total: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Documents (library)
# ============================================

"""Document represents a docgraph document with dependency tracking"""
type Document implements Node {
  """Document identifier (usually the relative path)"""
  id: ID!
  """Filesystem path relative to the library root"""
  path: String!
  """Human-readable title extracted from frontmatter"""
  title: String!
  """IDs of documents this document depends on"""
  dependsOn: [String!]!
  """IDs of input artifacts consumed by this document"""
  inputs: [String!]!
  """IDs of documents that depend on this document"""
  dependents: [String!]!
  """Review state metadata (null if never reviewed)"""
  review: DocumentReview
  """True if parked (excluded from staleness checks)"""
  parkingLot: Boolean!
  """Prompt text embedded in the document frontmatter"""
  prompt: String
  """Execution definition embedded in the document (null if none)"""
  execDef: DocumentExecDef
}

"""DocumentReview holds review-state metadata for a document"""
type DocumentReview {
  """Hash of the document content at review time"""
  selfHash: String!
  """Map of dependency IDs to their hashes at review time"""
  deps: JSON!
  """ISO-8601 timestamp of the last review"""
  reviewedAt: String!
}

"""DocumentExecDef holds an execution definition embedded in a document"""
type DocumentExecDef {
  """Definition identifier"""
  id: String
  """IDs of artifacts this definition produces or consumes"""
  artifactIds: [String!]
  """How to invoke the execution"""
  executor: ExecutorSpec
  """How to interpret the result"""
  result: ResultSpec
  """Evaluation criteria and thresholds"""
  evaluation: Evaluation
  """Whether the definition is currently active"""
  active: Boolean
  """Whether the definition must pass for merge"""
  required: Boolean
  """Whether this definition was discovered from the docgraph"""
  graphSource: Boolean
  """When the definition was created"""
  createdAt: DateTime
}

"""ExecutorSpec describes how to invoke an execution"""
type ExecutorSpec {
  """Execution kind (e.g. "shell", "agent")"""
  kind: String!
  """Command and arguments to run"""
  command: [String!]
  """Working directory for the command"""
  cwd: String
  """Environment variables as key-value pairs"""
  env: JSON
  """Maximum execution time in milliseconds"""
  timeoutMs: Int
}

"""ResultSpec describes how to interpret an execution result"""
type ResultSpec {
  """Metric-shaped projection (null if not metric-typed)"""
  metric: MetricResultSpec
}

"""MetricResultSpec defines metric extraction from execution output"""
type MetricResultSpec {
  """Unit of measurement (e.g. "ms", "bytes")"""
  unit: String
  """JSONPath to the numeric value"""

# DDx Agent Instructions

This repository uses DDx's built-in bead tracker for durable work management.

## Bead Policy

- Treat `.ddx/beads.jsonl` as DDx-managed data, not as a hand-edited document.
- Create beads only with `ddx bead create`.
- Modify bead metadata only with `ddx bead update`.
- Manage dependencies only with `ddx bead dep add` and `ddx bead dep remove`.
- Close work only with `ddx bead close`.
- Use `ddx bead import` and `ddx bead export` for bulk migration or interchange.
- Commit tracker mutations by default after bead commands.
- If the tracker change stands alone, make a tracker-only commit promptly.
- If it belongs to related implementation/docs work already being prepared for
  commit, fold the tracker change into that same commit instead of leaving
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` dirty.

## Prohibited Actions

- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.
- Do not add, remove, or rewrite bead rows with `apply_patch`, editors, scripts, or ad hoc JSONL manipulation.
- Do not invent bead IDs or prefixes such as `hx-*` or `ddx-*`.
- Do not treat nearby tracker entries as a naming pattern to copy.

## If The CLI Seems Insufficient

- Prefer the nearest supported `ddx bead` command.
- If the required tracker operation is not supported by `ddx bead`, stop and ask rather than editing tracker storage directly.

## Verification

- Use `ddx bead show <id>` to inspect one bead.
- Use `ddx bead list`, `ddx bead ready`, and `ddx bead status` to inspect queue state.
- Use `ddx bead --help` and `ddx bead create --help` before assuming a flag exists.

## Skill Policy

- Treat `SKILL.md` frontmatter as a strict interface, not freeform metadata.
- Published DDx skills must use top-level YAML frontmatter fields `name` and `description`.
- Use `argument-hint` only when the skill accepts a trailing positional or shorthand invocation hint.
- Do not use nested `skill:` frontmatter for DDx repo skills.
- Run `ddx skills check [path ...]` for reusable validation across repo skills and plugin skills.
- Run `make skill-schema` after editing any file under `skills/*/SKILL.md` or `cli/internal/skills/*/SKILL.md`.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.

DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output

## Architecture

This monorepo produces three artifacts:
- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site

### Key Components

- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
  - `main.go` - Application entry point
- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`)
  - `templates/` - Project templates
  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns
  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions
  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions
  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry
- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)
- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts

## Development Commands

### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)

```bash
# Build and test
make build          # Build for current platform
make test           # Run Go tests
make lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)
make fmt            # Format Go code

# Development workflow
make all            # Clean, deps, test, build
make dev            # Development mode with file watching (requires air)
make run ARGS="..."  # Run CLI with arguments
make install        # Install locally to ~/.local/bin/ddx

# Dependencies
make deps           # Install and tidy Go modules
make update-deps    # Update all dependencies

# Multi-platform builds
make build-all      # Build for all platforms
make release        # Create release archives
```

### Frontend Development (SvelteKit)

The web UI is a SvelteKit application built with Bun. Run from the repository root:

```bash
# Install frontend dependencies and start dev server
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun install && bun run dev

# Generate GraphQL types from schema
bun run houdini:generate

# Run unit tests
bun run test

# Run e2e tests with Playwright
bun run test:e2e
```

Frontend build output is embedded into the Go binary via `//go:embed`.

### Project Structure Navigation

The CLI uses git subtree for managing the relationship between individual projects and the master DDx repository. The `.ddx.yml` configuration file defines:
- Repository URL and branch
- Included resources (prompts, scripts, templates, patterns)
- Template variables and overrides
- Git subtree settings

### Key Patterns

1. **Command Structure**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate file in `cli/cmd/`
2. **Configuration Management**: Uses Viper for config file handling with YAML format
3. **Template Processing**: Variable substitution system for customizing templates
4. **Git Integration**: Built on git subtree for reliable version control and contribution workflows
5. **Cross-Platform Support**: Makefile supports building for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)

## Architectural Principles

**CRITICAL**: The DDx CLI follows the principle of "Extensibility Through Composition" - keep the CLI core minimal and add features through library resources.

1. **Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows**:
   - CLI owns: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
   - Workflow tools own: phase enforcement, supervisory loops, methodology-specific validation
   - DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions

2. **Feature Addition Pattern**:
   - Document-type resources go in the library (personas, patterns, templates)
   - Platform capabilities go in the CLI (beads, agent service, MCP)
   - Workflow-specific logic stays in workflow tools (HELIX, etc.)

## Testing and Quality

**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:

```bash
# Run full test suite - all tests must pass
cd cli && go test -v ./cmd
```

Every test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.

- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code
- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)
- Code formatting with `go fmt`
- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained

### Pre-commit Checks

The project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:

```bash
# Run all pre-commit checks
lefthook run pre-commit

# Or stage files and run checks
git add <files>
lefthook run pre-commit
```

Pre-commit checks include:
- Secrets detection
- Binary file prevention
- Debug statement detection
- Merge conflict detection
- DDx configuration validation
- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing

## CLI Command Overview

The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:

**Core Commands:**
- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project
- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues
- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version
- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository
- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community
- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status

**Bead Tracker:**
- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD
- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads
- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management
- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br

**Agent Service:**
- `ddx agent run --harness=<name> --prompt <file>` - Invoke an AI agent
- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus
- `ddx agent execute-bead <id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result
- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses
- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check
- `ddx agent log` - Session history

**Resource Commands:**
- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts
- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates
- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas
- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers

**Embedded Utilities:**
- `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required

## Persona System

DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:

- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `strict-code-reviewer`, `test-engineer-tdd`)
- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`

Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.

# System Instructions

**Execute ONLY what is requested:**

- **YAGNI** (You Aren't Gonna Need It): Implement only specified features. No "useful additions" or "while we're here" features.
- **KISS** (Keep It Simple, Stupid): Choose the simplest solution that meets requirements. Avoid clever code or premature optimization.
- **DOWITYTD** (Do Only What I Told You To Do): Stop when the task is complete. No extra refactoring, documentation, or improvements unless explicitly requested.

**Response Style:**
- Be concise and direct
- Skip preamble and postamble
- Provide complete information without unnecessary elaboration
- Stop immediately when the task is done

**When coding:**
- Write only code needed to pass tests
- No gold-plating or speculative features
- Follow existing patterns and conventions
- Add only requested functionality

Do what has been asked; nothing more, nothing less.
NEVER create files unless they're absolutely necessary for achieving your goal.
ALWAYS prefer editing an existing file to creating a new one.
NEVER proactively create documentation files (*.md) or README files unless the task explicitly requires creating or editing documentation.
